questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Quarter non-conducting disc of radius $4 R$ having uniform surface charge density $\sigma$ is placed in xz-plane then which of the following is the correct :
options: electric potential at $(0,3 R, 0)$ is $\frac{\sigma R}{4 \epsilon_0}$
electric potential at $(0,0,0)$ is $\frac{\sigma R}{2 \epsilon_0}$
electric field at $(0,3 R, 0)$ is symmetric with $x$ and $z$ axis
electric field intensity at $(-4 R, 0,-4 R)$ is equally inclined with $x$ and $z$ axis
questions.solution: (1) $v=\int_0^{4 R} \frac{\mathrm{~K} \frac{\pi}{2} \mathrm{rdr} \sigma}{\sqrt{\mathrm{r}^2+(3 R)^2}}=\frac{\sigma \mathrm{R}}{4 \epsilon_0}$ (2) $v=\int_0^{4 r} \frac{K \frac{\pi}{2} r d r \sigma}{r}=\frac{\sigma R}{2 \epsilon_0}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A positive charge $q$ is placed inside a neutral hollow conducting sphere of radius $R$, as shown in figure. Whole system is placed in uniform external vertical electric field pointing downward (line PCQ is also vertical) then select the correct statement(s) about electric field at point $P$. Point $P$ is a point of the material inside the conductor.
options: Electric field due to outer surface of sphere at point $P$ is zero
Magnitude of electric field due to inner surface of sphere at point $P$ is $\frac{q}{9 \pi \epsilon_0 R^2}$
Magnitude of electric field at point $P$, due to hollow sphere is less than $E$
Direction of resultant electric field at point $P$ due to charge $q$ and hollow sphere is vertically upward
questions.solution: External electric field and induced charge on outer surface of the sphere will produce a net zero field inside it. Net field at $P$ must be vertically upward.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Three identical point charges are placed on the vertices of an equilateral triangle ABC as shown in the figure. Consider a median AD , where point O is the centroid. As we move form point $A$ to point $D$
options: Electric field vanishes only once at O, between points A and D.
Electric field vanishes two times, first at O and then somewhere between O \& D .
Electric field vanishes two times, first at a point somewhere between $\mathrm{A} \& \mathrm{O}$ and then at O .
Electric field vector changes direction more than once.
questions.solution: $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{A}}=$ field due to charge on $\mathrm{A} ; \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{AB}}=$ field due to charges on B and C
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Figure shows four charges fixed on the vertex of a square in horizontal plane. A charged bead is constrained to move along a wire which passes through centre of square \& perpendicular to its plane. Mark the correct statement(s).
options: In gravity free space a positively charged bead cannot be in stable equilibrium at any position.
In gravity free space a negatively charged bead cannot be in stable equilibrium at any position.
In presence of gravity a negatively charged bead cannot be in stable equilibrium at any position above the plane of fixed charges.
In presence of gravity a positively charged bead cannot be in stable equilibrium position above the plane of fixed charges.
questions.solution: In space (gravity not zero) net force on $+q$ is vertically upward. So that equilibrium can be achieved in the space. If charge is $-q$ then net force on it is vertically downward so equilibrium not achieved.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A thin-walled, spherical conducting shell S of radius R is given charge Q . The same amount of charge is also placed at its centre C . Which of the following statement(s) are correct?
options: On the outer surface of S , the charge density is $\frac{Q}{2 \pi R^2}$.
The electric field is zero at all points inside S .
At a point just outside $S$, the electric field is double the field at a point just inside $S$.
At any point inside S , the electric field is inversely proportional to the square of its distance from centre C .
questions.solution: -Q charge must be induced on the inner surface of the shell. Hence $+2 Q$ charge appears on its outer surface.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The following figure shows a block of mass m suspended from a fixed point by means of a vertical spring. The block is oscillating simple harmonically and carries a charge q . There also exists a uniform electric field in the region. Consider four different cases. The electric field is zero, in case-1, $\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{q}}$ downward in case-2, $\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{q}}$ upward in case- 3 and $\frac{2 \mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{q}}$ downward in case-4. The speed at mean position is same in all cases. Select the correct alternative(s).
options: Time periods of oscillation are equal in case-1 and case-3
Amplitudes of displacement are same in case-2 and case-3
The maximum elongation (increment in length from natural length) is maximum in case-4
Time periods of oscillation are equal in case- 2 and case-4
questions.solution:
(1) Only equilibrium position changes. Time period remains same $\mathrm{T}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{~m}}{\mathrm{k}}}$.
(2) $\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kx}^2=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2 \quad \therefore \mathrm{v}$ at mean position is same amplitude will be same.
(3) In case -4 Eaquilibrium position $\mathrm{x}_0=\frac{3 \mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{k}}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two point charges $+q$ and $-q$ are fixed on diametrically opposite point on the uniform ring, such that -q is at bottom and is in contact with a perfect insulator incline plane. Total mass is equal to m . It remains in equilibrium on the rough inclined plane in the presence of uniform vertical electric field $\vec{E}$. Then
options: the value of friction force is $\mathrm{mg} \sin \theta$.
the value of friction force is less than $\mathrm{mg} \sin \theta$.
the value of electric field is $\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{2 \mathrm{q}}$.
the value of electric field is $\frac{m g \tan \theta}{2 q}$.
questions.solution:
$f=m g \sin \theta$
and $P E \sin \theta=m g \sin \theta . \mathrm{R}$
$q 2 R E \sin \theta=m g \sin \theta R$
$\mathrm{E}=\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{2 \mathrm{q}} \quad \therefore \quad$ Ans. $\quad(\mathrm{1}) \&(\mathrm{3})$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A large insulating thick sheet of thickness 2d carries a uniform charge per unit volume $\rho$. A particle of mass m , carrying a charge q having a sign opposite to that of the sheet, is released from the surface of the sheet. The sheet does not offer any mechanical resistance to the motion of the particle. Find the oscillation frequency $v$ of the particle inside the sheet.
options: $v=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{q} \mathrm{\rho}}{\mathrm{~m} \varepsilon_0}}$
$v=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{2 q \rho}{m \varepsilon_0}}$
$v=\frac{1}{4 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{q} \rho}{\mathrm{m} \varepsilon_0}}$
$v=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{q} \mathrm{\rho}}{\mathrm{~m} \varepsilon_0}}$
questions.solution: It we draw a cuboid Gausian surface of lateral cross section $A$ and height $h$ and width $2 x$ where $\mathrm{x}=$ distance from central line then E will be pointing as shown (by symmetry) $\begin{aligned} & \phi_{\mathrm{g}}=\oint \mathrm{E} \cdot \mathrm{ds}=2 \mathrm{EA}=\frac{\mathrm{q}_{\text {encl }}}{\varepsilon_0} \text { (Flux through lateral surface if } \mathrm{E}=\text { Electric field at distance } \mathrm{x} \\ & \mathrm{q}_{\text {enclosed }}=[(\ell)(2 \mathrm{x})(\mathrm{h})] \rho \\ & 2 \mathrm{E} \mathrm{~A}=\frac{\mathrm{q}_{\text {enclosed }}}{\varepsilon_0}=\frac{2 \mathrm{Ax} \rho}{\varepsilon_0}=\frac{(2 \mathrm{x})(\ell) \mathrm{h} \rho}{\varepsilon_0}=\frac{2 \mathrm{xA} \rho}{\varepsilon_0}[\text { as } \ell \mathrm{h}=\mathrm{A}] \end{aligned}$ So $\mathrm{E}=-\frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0} \mathrm{x}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { Force }=\mathrm{qE}=-\frac{\mathrm{q} \rho}{\varepsilon_0} \mathrm{x} \\ & \mathrm{~m} \frac{\mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{dt}^2}=\frac{-\mathrm{q} \rho}{\varepsilon_0} \mathrm{x} \Rightarrow \omega^2=\frac{\mathrm{q} \rho}{\varepsilon_0 \mathrm{~m}} \\ & \omega=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{q} \rho}{\varepsilon_0 \mathrm{~m}}} ; v=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{q} \rho}{\mathrm{~m} \varepsilon_0}} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle of mass $m$ and charge $-q$ has been projected from ground as shown in the figure below. Mark out the correct statements (s) :-
options: The path of motion of the particle is parabolic
The path of motion of the particle is a straight line
Time of flight of particle is $\frac{2 u \sin \theta}{g}$
Range of motion of the particle will be less than $\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g}$
questions.solution:
Along X axis, $\mathrm{u}_{\mathrm{x}}=\mathrm{u} \cos \theta, \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{x}}=-\frac{\mathrm{qE}}{\mathrm{m}} \quad$ Along Y axis, $\mathrm{u}_{\mathrm{y}}=\mathrm{u} \sin \theta, \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{y}}=-\mathrm{g}$
Equations of motion along $X$ and $Y$ axes would be $x=u \cos \theta t-\frac{q E}{2 m} t^2$ and $y=u \sin \theta t-\frac{1}{2} g t^2$
Time of flight remains unchanged as vertical motion is not affected by $\vec{E}$. Range of the particle in the present case is always less than $\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{\mathrm{~g}}$ whatever be the value of
E. $\mathrm{R}=\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{T}}=\mathrm{u} \cos \theta \times \frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{g}}-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{\mathrm{qE}}{\mathrm{m}}\left[\frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{g}}\right]^2 \lt \frac{\mathrm{u}^2 \sin 2 \theta}{\mathrm{~g}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a region of space, the electric field $\vec{E}=E_0 x \hat{i}+E_0 y \hat{j}$. Consider an imaginary cubical volume of edge ' $a$ ' with its edges parallel to the axes of coordinates. Now,
options: the total electric flux through the faces 1 and 3 is $E_0 a^3$
the charge inside the cubical volume is $2 \varepsilon_0 \mathrm{E}_0 \mathrm{a}^3$
the total electric flux through the faces 2 and 4 is $2 \mathrm{E}_0 \mathrm{a}^3$
the charge inside the cubical volume is $\varepsilon_0 \mathrm{E}_0 \mathrm{a}^3$ON
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \phi=\vec{E} \cdot \vec{A} \\ & \phi_{\text {in }}=-v e \text { and } \phi_{\text {out }}=+v e \\ & Q_{\text {net }}=\frac{q_{\text {in }}}{\varepsilon_0} \\ & \phi_1=0, \phi_2=E_0 a^3, \phi_3=E_0 a^3, \phi_4=0, \phi_5=0, \phi_6=0 \\ & Q_{\text {net }}=\phi_1+\phi_2+\phi_3+\phi_4+\phi_5+\phi_6=2 E_0 a^3 \Rightarrow 2 E_0 a^3=\frac{q_{\text {in }}}{\in_0} \Rightarrow q_{\text {in }}=2 \varepsilon_0 E_0 a^3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
